steps.title: Apply the sum of interior angles formula
steps.content:
For a polygon with $n$ sides, the sum of all interior angles is $(n-2) \times 180°$. For an 87-sided polygon:
$$\text{Sum} = (87-2) \times 180° = 85 \times 180°$$

steps.title: Find the measure of each interior angle
steps.content:
Since the problem states that all 87 angles are equal (each measures $180p°$), divide the total sum by 87:
$$\text{Each angle} = \frac{85 \times 180°}{87}$$

steps.title: Solve for p
steps.content:
Set the expression for each angle equal to $180p°$:
$$\frac{85 \times 180}{87} = 180p$$
Divide both sides by 180:
$$p = \frac{85}{87}$$
In decimal form, $p \approx 0.977$ or $0.9770$.

Equivalent forms accepted: 85/87, 0.977, .9770.
